title: Word Break
problem_statement: Given a string and a word dictionary, determine if the string can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of dictionary words.

examples.explanation: The string can be segmented as "leet code".

intuition: Use DP to check at each index if a dictionary word ends there and previous prefix is valid.

approaches.name: DP over string prefixes
approaches.description: dp[i] is true if s[0..i-1] can be segmented; extend using words from the dictionary.
approaches.why_not: Brute force segmentation tries all splits and duplicates prefix checks.

interview_qa.q: What is the meaning of dp[i] in Word Break?
interview_qa.a: It means the prefix s[0..i-1] can be segmented into dictionary words.

interview_qa.q: How can checking dictionary words be optimized?
interview_qa.a: Use a hash set for constant-time word lookup and limit substring lengths by the max word length.
